item.title: Today’s Contexto: Hints & Answer For June 11, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Contexto is a word association game that gives you a new word to guess every single day. With every attempt, the AI algorithm determines how close your guess is to the daily answer based on thematic and contextual similarity.

Sometimes, though, no matter how experienced you are, the word you’re looking for can be incredibly vague and hard to deduce. Luckily for you, this guide is full of hints to give you a helping hand, but make sure you give today’s Contexto a go if you haven’t already!

How To Play Contexto

Contexto is incredibly simple to play, but difficult to master. All you have to do is type words into the search bar, and the AI algorithm will tell you how close your word is to the answer.

You’ll know how close your guess is based on the colour of the bar and the number next to it.

  • Red includes guesses with the number 1,501 and above, meaning you’re way off from the secret word.
  • Orange includes guesses between 301 and 1,500, meaning you’re getting closer, but still some ways off.
  • Green includes guesses between 1 and 300, meaning you’re on the right track.

Best Contexto Tips & Tricks

  • Start off using broad terms, then narrow down once you’ve got under the 1,000 mark.
  • Try using Nouns first, and dip into verbs or adjectives once you’ve discovered the context.
  • Experiment with random words at times, especially as different forms of a root word can rank differently, such as work and worker.
  • Guess away, as unlike other word games like Wordle, you aren’t limited to a strict number of attempts.

Best Starting Words For Contexto

Here are some great starting words to get you going with Contexto:

  • Thing
  • Person
  • Place
  • Animal
  • Work
  • Nature
  • Game
  • Concept
  • Idea
  • Food

Contexto Hints For Today, June 11, 2026

Without giving away today’s Contexto answer, here are a few helpful hints to get you thinking in the right direction:

  • Hint 1: Type of cake
  • Hint 2: Bottom layer is biscuit
  • Hint 3: __________ Factory

If they don’t help, then here are the nine closest words, going from tenth to second:

  • Cake
  • Fudge
  • Pudding
  • Muffin
  • Pie
  • Dessert
  • Cupcake
  • Brownie
  • Tiramisu

Today’s Contexto Answer For June 11, 2026

If you’ve tried everything and still can’t figure out today’s Contexto answer, then you can find it down below:

Answer: Cheesecake

A Cheesecake is a dessert made from a layer of biscuit, topped with soft cheese, eggs, sugar and typically fruit.

When Is The Next Contexto Game?

Contexto is updated daily at 12 AM or Midnight in your respective time zone, meaning the next game will start on June 12

The Contexto schedule is very consistent, with a new game releasing at the same time every day.

How did you find today’s Contexto? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

Charlie is one of Insider Gaming's Guides Writers who's been in the gaming industry for a little over a year. Unfortunately for him, he enjoys every video game genre out…

item.title: Crimson Desert Smashes 6 Million Sales in 83 Days as Pearl Abyss Beats Every Korean Single-Player Record
item.content:encoded:
Today, South Korean developer Pearl Abyss revealed that Crimson Desert has now sold over six million units in less than three months from the game's March 19 launch. Jin-young Heo, CEO of Pearl Abyss, said in a statement:

We are sincerely grateful to players around the world for helping Crimson Desert achieve meaningful success in the global market despite being a new IP. To provide more enjoyable and better experiences, Pearl Abyss will continue moving forward and taking on new challenges.

The open world action/adventure title sold 2 million copies on launch day and exceeded 5 million within a month, ultimately reaching 6 million in just 83 days. Pearl Abyss is all too happy to highlight that this is an unprecedented sales achievement for a single player game developed in Korea. They also cited recent data from U.S. market research firm Circana, which showed Crimson Desert ranked No. 2 in U.S. video game sales year to date in 2026.

This new sales milestone comes as Pearl Abyss continues to enhance the game on a regular basis. A series of content and feature updates will be gradually released from June to September, further boosting Crimson Desert's appeal to both current players and new audiences. These updates will feature story enhancements, new combat elements, cross-save functionality across PC, PlayStation, and Xbox, along with various quality-of-life improvements. Recently, PR and Marketing Director Will Powers claimed these patches are basically free marketing for the game.

Pearl Abyss has also announced that downloadable content (DLC) is in the works, with more information to be revealed in the future. For more about Crimson Desert, head to our roundup page.

About the author: With over two decades of experience in gaming journalism, Alessio Palumbo has led the gaming vertical at Wccftech since August 2015. He started working at a young age for Italian websites like Everyeye.it, Gamestar.it, Nextgame.it, and Multiplayer.it before kickstarting the indie English-language publication Worlds Factory as its founder and Editor in Chief. In the last decade, he has coordinated the overall output of Wccftech's gaming section, managed PR relations, assigned reviews, produced daily news coverage, edited gaming content as needed, and delivered game reviews. Arguably, his trademark content is the long series of exclusive developer interviews that have been cited by Wikipedia and by the biggest news media and gaming publications. His passion for technology also makes him knowledgeable when it comes to gaming hardware and tech. His favorite genres include RPGs, MMORPGs, and action/adventure games.

Follow Wccftech on Google to get more of our news coverage in your feeds.

item.title: The Elder Scrolls VI Finally Gets a Status Check —From Xbox’s Matt Booty — Eight Years After Its First Teaser
item.content:encoded:
As of yesterday, exactly eight years have passed from that Bethesda E3 2018 when Todd Howard showed teasers for both The Elder Scrolls VI and Starfield. That move was specifically geared toward reassuring Bethesda fans that they weren't abandoning single player games with the release of the multiplayer game Fallout 76. However, in the years to come, Howard would make it clear that he really prefers not to talk about any of his games until they're relatively close to launch. In fact, he'd actually rather show them and then say 'they're available now', which happened with The Elder Scrolls IV: Oblivion Remastered.

That, of course, clashes with the marketing plans of Bethesda and Microsoft's Xbox, which bought Bethesda back in 2021. On that note, it's perhaps not much of a surprise that an update on The Elder Scrolls VI landed yesterday from Xbox Chief Content Officer Matt Booty, who confirmed to Variety that he had seen the game and that it's looking "amazing" so far.

I would say one of the more challenging balancing acts of someone in a job like mine is balancing that you want to go show the world all the cool stuff you’re working on, and you want to get them excited early, but we also know that we want to wait till the right moment. And when you decide to show it, you want it to be the best you’ve got. And also that when you show the game, you’re also giving them a promise of, hey, it’s coming soon. So I can tell you, having visited Bethesda and sat with Todd and seen “Elder Scrolls” playing, it looks amazing, and it’s coming along well. And we’ll make sure to announce it and really reveal it at the right time.

The Elder Scrolls VI was never going to be a 2026 game. It might be a 2027 game, though that'd be a bit surprising, given that Starfield launched in September 2023. A few months ago, Todd Howard said it would be "a while yet", while he confirmed that they're pushing the technology further with the Creation Engine 3 and that they were happy with how that's going.

For more about TES VI, check out our dedicated roundup page.

About the author: With over two decades of experience in gaming journalism, Alessio Palumbo has led the gaming vertical at Wccftech since August 2015. item.title: Kyoto Xanadu details more characters, requests, cursed armor, and conversation events
item.content:encoded:
Falcom has released new information and screenshots for action RPG Kyoto Xanadu introducing characters Wyatt Warren and Marina Yuino, as well as the various requests players can accept, cursed armor, and conversation events.

Get the details below.

■ Characters

Wyatt Warren (voiced by Ryuho Nagaoka)

  • Age: 18

A brilliant third-year student who serves as the receptionist of Hirasaka Academy’s Research Building.

A member dispatched from an American company affiliated with the Zodiac Corporate Alliance, he is neither a member of Suruga’s team nor one of his subordinates.

While he speaks in an administrative manner, he occasionally invites Rei to participate in questionable experiments, and his quick-wittedness also causes him to sometimes jump to wild conclusions.

Marina Yuino

  • Age: 15

A female student who serves as the receptionist of Hirasaka Academy’s chapel.

She deeply admires Luana, who is sometimes known as the “Saint of Siena,” and is also a member of the Nameless Fruit team led by Luana.

Although she is often absent-minded and prone to mistakes, she studies hard every day in hopes of becoming a full-fledged sister.

■ Systems: Facility Requests

By accepting requests from the various facilities at Hirasaka Academy and completing them within the labyrinth before returning, players can earn “rewards” and “contribution points” from the academy.

Requests from the Research Building

Requests to obtain “Otherworldly Materials” can be completed by destroying special environmental objects found within the labyrinth.

Requests from the Training Ground

Subjugation requests can be completed by defeating a specific number of monster within the labyrinth.

Requests from the Chapel

Rescue requests from the chapel can be completed by rescuing lost students who are stranded in “Uncertain Domain.”

■ Systems: Kijaku Tenjinguu

While exploring the labyrinth, players may obtain “cursed armor.”

After returning to the real world, cursed armor can be dispelled at Kijaku Tenjinguu on Shijo Street in Kyoto, restoring it into special armor with unique properties.

The process requires “Dispel Talismans,” which can be purchased at Kijaku Tenjinguu.

■ Systems: Tankasho / Kien Kobanashi

While exploring Hirasaka Academy and Kyoto, special events called “Tankasho” and “Kienkobanashi” may occur at specific locations which will deepen the player’s bond with various characters.

By completing these events, players can earn “relationship points.” Once enough points have been obtained, special events will occur. Maxing out a character’s relationship points will award players rare items as a “token of friendship.”

Tankasho

Tankasho depicts conversations with main characters such as Fuka, Kazuki, Sena, homeroom teacher Tatsumiya, and others.

Kien Kobanashi

Kien Kobanashi depicts the individual stories of various students who attend Hirasaka Academy.

■ Kien Kobanashi Characters

Mitsutaka Kino (voiced by Kohei Yanagi)

  • Age: 18
  • Soul Device: Jitte types

A second-year student at Hirasaka Academy from Miyagi Prefecture.

He greatly admires tokusatsu heroes, and after awakening as a Chosen, enrolled at the academy in pursuit of becoming a “true hero.”

However, his naturally timid and pessimistic personality often leads him to worry that he is holding his team back.

Sayuri Shinozuka (voiced by Riko Akechi)

  • Age: 17
  • Soul Device: Japanese parasol types

A second-year student at Hirasaka Academy from Gifu Prefecture.

As the daughter of a prestigious Chosen family, she is generally well mannered. However, she has a sharp personality and is particularly harsh toward Rei.

In combat, she specializes in using a Japanese parasol-type Soul Device and serves as the ace of a mid-ranked team at the academy.

Komachi Takigawa (voiced by Yuno Azuma)

  • Age: 16
  • Soul Device: Hammer types

A cheerful and friendly first-year student from Fukuoka Prefecture.

She has an enormous appetite and often gets caught up in lively conversations about food-related topics.

Although she hails from a line of Chosen, her family is not a traditional prestigious household, having instead been raised in a more common middle class environment.

Soshi Akazome (voiced by Kosuke Kuwano)

  • Age: 16
  • Soul Device: Oni hand types

A one-armed first-year student from Aichi Prefecture.

His Soul Device manifests as a giant oni hand that makes up for his lack of a left arm, allowing him to tear through monsters in battle.

He appears to avoid interacting with others and is almost always alone.

Rikiya Nito (voiced by Yoshinori Nakayama)

  • Age: 18
  • Soul Device: Kanabo types

A physically adept third-year student from Nara Prefecture.

His bold and energetic personality can sometimes overwhelm those around him, but he is extremely disciplined when it comes to honing his body.

He enrolled at Hirasaka Academy to gain the strength needed to fight monsters, and currently serves as the leader of one of the academy’s top-ranked athletics teams.

Tomoe Furube (voiced by Yuzuki Watase)

  • Age: 19
  • Soul Device: Staff types

A refined third-year student from Nagasaki Prefecture who loves to read.

She belongs to a mid-ranked all-girls team at Hirasaka Academy.

She is somewhat of a neat freak, and stays on high alert when it comes to Rei due to the various rumors that have circulated about him since his transfer to the academy.

Kyoto Xanadu is due out for PlayStation 5, Switch 2, Switch, and PC via Steam on July 16 in Japan and Asia. A western release is also planned.



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/06/kyoto-xanadu-details-more-characters-requests-cursed-armor-and-conversation-events

item.title: Kalanoro launches August 27
item.content:encoded:
Save the World. Start a Band. Easy.

Welcome to Lemuria, a vibrant island world under the not-so-chill rule of the witch Raneny, who has decided because apparently dictatorship wasn’t enough, to throw a concert featuring the island’s greatest artists in her own honor.

Enter Kalakely, a small and fluffy Kalanoro from Malagasy folklore, and a slightly unwilling hero chosen by a very questionable prophecy to save the day.

Armed with mystical powers (and some surprisingly effective household items), Kalakely sets off on a wild journey across Lemuria to recruit legendary musicians, build a band, and overthrow tyranny… one gig at a time.

Hair Today, Gone Tomorrow (Enemies, That Is)

In Kalanoro, you’ll bounce from platforming to combat to band-building as you embark on an unexpected musical roadtrip designed to deliver pure good vibes.

You’ll make your way through lively biomes, jump over industrial traps, and push through handcrafted levels built around movement, hazards, and momentum. Along the way, you’ll fight with hair-powered abilities and whatever happens to be within reach—slippers and frying pans included. Boss encounters are built on that same spirit, leaning into the groovier, more theatrical side of Kalanoro‘s joyful chaos. As you progress, you’ll unlock new abilities, refine your playstyle, and embrace the game’s signature mix of “joyful chaos” and creative freedom.

A Musical Roadtrip Like No Other

But fighting is only half the adventure. As you travel across Lemuria, you’ll rescue eccentric lemur artists to grow the band and its popularity one stop at a time.

You’ll keep the crew rolling aboard a rusty old taxi-brousse that serves as both home base and tour bus. Between major beats, you will need to bond with your new companions through mini-games, manage the band’s happiness, and build toward your next big performance.

The more the band grows, the more the journey starts to feel like what Kalanoro really is at heart: a feel-good rebellion powered by collective energy, creativity, and one killer setlist.

That musical journey is brought to life by a soundtrack featuring incredible Malagasy artists such as Denise, Jyeuhair, and Marco Klarck, who embraced the project with real generosity and helped shape its spirit.

A Celebration of Malagasy Culture

At its heart, Kalanoro is a vibrant tribute to Malagasy folklore, music, and artistic culture. Set in a colorful fictional world, the game blends traditional influences with a playful, modern tone, brought to life through colorful hand-drawn visuals and dynamic animation powered by Unreal Engine.

Behind it all is Red Raketa Studio, an indie team based in Madagascar. Founded in 2021, the studio brings together a new generation of Malagasy talent whose voice and perspective help give Kalanoro its distinctive spirit.

Watch a new trailer below.



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/06/kalanoro-launches-august-27

item.title: NYT Strands #830 Hints and Answers for Today, June 11, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Strands is a daily word search puzzle game from The New York Times. It is a relatively new game, launched in 2024, in which players seek a common theme among words in a 6×8 letter grid.

Whether it’s your first time playing or you’re a Strands veteran, today’s tips and strategies can help you find words faster. So, if you’ve found yourself stuck with today’s Strands, don’t worry; below you’ll find some helpful hints and even the answers for Strands Game #830 for Thursday, June 11, 2026

To play today’s Strands, head to the game’s website.

IMPORTANT: The following information includes spoilers on today’s edition of Strands. If you don’t want the answer given to you, stop scrolling.

How To Play Strands

Strands is a daily word search that challenges you to find words with a common theme. There’s also a Spanagram that reveals the deeper theme behind the words.

  • Words that are three letters or fewer do not count.
  • Words that reveal the correct theme appear in blue.
  • The Spanagram turns yellow when found.
  • Finding three words that don’t fit the theme will reveal a hint to help you find the proper words.

The goal is to string together as many words as possible. Every letter on the grid gets used! Daily puzzles keep players coming back for more, making it a fun brain exercise and a popular online trend.

Strands can only be played on the New York Times website or on the NYT Games app.

Strands Hints for Today, June 11, 2026

The Strands’ answers to the theme of Game #830 are all words rhyming with ooze.

General Strands Hints for June 11

Without giving today’s Strands answers completely away, here are a few helpful hints for today to get you started:

  • Hint 1: Converse, Air Jordan, Crocs.
  • Hint 2: A music TV channel that launched in the 2000s, initially focusing on Alt Rock.
  • Hint 3: Kids show: ___ Clues.

Use these clues to narrow down your searches and increase your chances of solving Strands #830 for June 11, 2026.

Words to Help You Find Strand Hints for June 11 2026

If you want help getting started without having today’s Strands answer given to you, there are a few starter words worth trying that will help you unlock some in-game hints. A few starter words that may help are:

  • OOZE
  • RITE
  • YULES
  • HEMS
  • YULE

Today’s Strands Answer for Game #830 on June 11, 2026

If you’ve tried everything, including using the above hints, and are still stuck, you can find the answers to today’s Strands below. Check out the answers for past puzzles here.

  • SCHMOOZE
  • FUSE
  • CRUISE
  • BREWS
  • BLUES
  • CHOOSE
  • SHOES
  • SPANAGRAM: RHYMETIME

If you solved today’s Strands without our answers, congratulations! If not, take note of the best tips above for tomorrow’s puzzle, and check back daily for more hints.

When Is The Next Strands Game After #830?

All New York Times games are updated daily at 12 am or Midnight in your respective time zone. Information about the next game is often available in the hours leading up to it, giving players a heads-up. The game on June 12 will be Strands Game #830

What Is Strands?

Strands is a daily word search puzzle game created by The New York Times. Launched in 2024, Strands challenges players to discover a common theme among words in a 6×8 letter grid. The game’s original concept was developed by Juliette Seive, a research director on The New York Times Games team, with the aim of replicating Wordle’s success. Tracy Bennett has served as the puzzle editor for Strands, overseeing the creation and curation of daily puzzles since the publication’s launch.

For even more Insider Gaming delivered straight to your inbox, sign up for our weekly newsletter.

item.title: Ninjala Returns With An Open-World Sequel Exclusive To Switch 2 In 2027
item.content:encoded:
"The first half of a two part saga"

One of the many surprises of the latest Nintendo Direct was the announcement Ninjala would be making a comeback next year with a sequel.

Ninjala 2: The Uncharted Planet will arrive "exclusively" for the Switch 2 in Spring 2027, and promises to delivers the series' "first-ever open world". It's also described as being the "first chapter of a two-part saga", where the protagonist is "hurled into an unknown world after breaking a forbidden seal".

Here's a bit more about what to expect, courtesy of the official PR:

"Adventure across vast landscapes on a quest to return to Earth. Master diverse Ninja Gum actions like Gum Boost, Gum Balloon, and a new Gum Hook to speed across the planet, and score exhilarating “IPPON” victories against enemies with weapons like katanas, hammers, and rockets!"

There will also be four-player support for co-op, where players can battle against fearsome and giant foes locally and online. There's no mention of traditional competitive multiplayer (at least yet), or information about if the sequel will be paid or free-to-play like the previous game, but if we hear any updates, we'll let you know.

GungHo began winding down the original multiplayer-driven Ninjala game last year. The title also received a compatibility update on the Switch 2.

To celebrate the announcement of the second game, the first title is currently giving away 1,000 Jala for free for a limited time. Episodes 1-63 of the Ninjala anime are also returning to YouTube. GungHo notes how this "original series ties directly into the story of Ninjala 2", and if you catch up now, you'll be "walking into the new game like a true veteran".

Would you be interested in playing Ninjala 2? Did you play the first game? Let us know in the comments.

Liam is a news writer and reviewer on Nintendo Life. He's been writing about games for more than 15 years and is a lifelong fan of many iconic video game characters.

item.title: Mousebusters coming to Xbox One, Switch on June 25
item.content:encoded:
• Switch
  • Xbox One

"Cute and spooky horror adventure" game out today for PC.

Odencat will release “cute and spooky horror adventure” game Mousebusters for Xbox One and Switch on June 25, the developer announced.

The PC version launched today via Steam for $12.99.

Here is an overview of the game, via Odencat:

In this cute and spooky horror adventure, our protagonist moves into an apartment complex and is suddenly transformed into a mouse! The protagonist must team up with the mysterious mentor only known as the Chief to exorcise the evil spirits lurking throughout the building.

Looking at the world through the eyes of mice, players will explore the rooms of the building’s colorful residents and corner the evil spirits through various interactive challenges and minigames. Armed with a raygun from the Chief, players will participate in EEB (Exciting Exorcism Battles) to exorcise spirits, cancelling out their attacks while targeting their weak points..

Watch a new trailer below.

item.title: Crimson Desert sales top six million
item.content:encoded:
• PC
  • PS5
  • Xbox Series

One million units sold since April 15.

Total sales for open-world action adventure game Crimson Desert have surpassed six million units, developer Pearl Abyss announced.

“Your adventures and tales continue to make the journey of Crimson Desert even more special,” Pearl Abyss aid. “Once again, thank you to every Greymane who has stepped into Pywel and experienced the world of Crimson Desert in their own way.”

Crimson Desert launched for PlayStation 5, Xbox Series, and PC via Steam and Epic Games Store on March 19. It surpassed two million sales just one day after release, three million units by March 24, four million units by March 31, and five million units by April 15.

item.title: Wizardry 45th anniversary main visual unveiled
item.content:encoded:
Created by illustrator Jun Suemi.

Drecom has unveiled the main visual for Wizardry‘s 4th anniversary, which was created by illustrator Jun Suemi.

Additionally, a Wizardry 45th anniversary special survey is now underway, where fans can share their memories and messages regarding Wizardry.

Get the details below.

The Main Visual for the 45th Anniversary Project, Created by Illustrator Jun Suemi, Has Been Unveiled!

To kick off the 45th anniversary, the official Wizardry 45th Anniversary Special Site was recently launched. Following the release of a video message from co-creator Robert Woodhead, we are thrilled to finally reveal the main visual created by illustrator, Jun Suemi.

This illustration connects a 45 year history with an expanding path into the future. It stands as a stunning visual featuring a luxurious crossover of characters that color the series, including those from the 3D dungeon RPG Wizardry Variants Daphne and the novel Blade & Bastard. In tandem with this reveal, the main visual of the special site has been fully updated.

Every adventurer who visits the site will now be welcomed by Suemi’s overwhelmingly immersive worldview. We invite you to feast your eyes on this masterpiece that seamlessly connects the past and future of Wizardry.

Your “Memories of Adventure” Will Light Up the 45th Anniversary! Special Survey Now Live!

We are excited to launch the Wizardry 45th Anniversary Special Survey on our official site.

For 45 years since its birth, countless daring adventures have been woven by players all around the world. We would love to hear your personal thoughts and passion for Wizardry—whether it’s a nostalgic memory of venturing deep into the “Dungeon” in the past, or an episode that remains deeply etched in your heart to this day.

We also welcome your enthusiastic messages of support for the future of the franchise, including our latest title Wizardry Variants Daphne and Blade & Bastard.

The precious memories and warm messages you share will serve as a cherished chronicle to elevate our 45th anniversary project, inspiring us as we move forward into the future.

We look forward to hearing from all you adventurers!

Response Deadline: August 31, 2026 at 23:59 JST.



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/06/wizardry-45th-anniversary-main-visual-unveiled

item.title: Square Enix Releases Final Fantasy XIV Online Switch 2 Gameplay Trailer
item.content:encoded:
Early access begins this August

During the Nintendo Direct this week, Square Enix's upcoming release Final Fantasy XIV Online featured in a sizzle reel towards the end of the presentation.

Although it was only on display for a moment, it was arguably the best look at this version of the game yet. Now, Square Enix has released a gameplay trailer of the online multiplayer title running on the Switch 2. It's got some quick segments of gameplay, mixed with in-game cutscenes.

In case you missed it, this title will be getting an "early access" release on the Switch 2 in August 2026. Here are some additional details from Square Enix's support page about the early access period, or if you're a player coming across from another platform:

Those playing FINAL FANTASY XIV for the first time on Nintendo Switch 2
Purchase FINAL FANTASY XIV for Nintendo Switch 2 and a subscription to play the game.

Current FINAL FANTASY XIV players who wish to play on Nintendo Switch 2
If you already play FINAL FANTASY XIV on another platform, you will still need to purchase FINAL FANTASY XIV for Nintendo Switch 2 and a separate subscription for the Nintendo Switch 2 version of the game. However, if you have remaining game time on other platforms, the Nintendo Switch 2 subscription will be available at half price.

There will be an approximately one-month period of early access to ensure server stability, after which official service will start. A subscription to Nintendo Switch Online will not be required to play FINAL FANTASY XIV on Nintendo Switch 2 in either the early access period or during official service.

During the latest Nintendo Direct, Square Enix also announced the new HD-2D title, Final Fantasy Resonance for Switch and Switch 2. It's out this October.

Will you be checking out FFXIV when it arrives on Switch 2 in August? Let us know in the comments.

Liam is a news writer and reviewer on Nintendo Life. He's been writing about games for more than 15 years and is a lifelong fan of many iconic video game characters.

item.title: Hades II Is Getting Another Update Soon, Here Are The Full Patch Notes
item.content:encoded:
More fixes are on the way

Supergiant Games has already released some patches for Hades II, and even more are on the way. The latest release on the way is the "Hotfix 4 Patch".

According to the team, these fixes will be "coming to console versions down the line", so when we hear more about this update for the Switch and Switch 2 versions we'll provide an update. Thankfully, it is only a small list of changes.

"We just released a Hotfix with a handful of additional fixes to our latest Hades II Post-Launch Patch! These fixes will be coming to console versions down the line. See you at the Crossroads!"

Here's the full rundown via Steam:

Post-Launch Patch 2 - Hotfix4

·Fixed Suffering on Sight (Medea) sometimes damaging foes already present in new Locations
· Fixed Tall Order (Hermes) providing its bonus even when you did not meet the Element requirement
· Fixed a case where Artemis could be singing by the Crossroads Cauldron rather than in her usual spot
· Fixed Dire Auto-Seekers becoming immune to being stunned if their Armor was depleted in their defensive mode
· Fixed indicator remaining visible during Melinoë's Return to Shadow sequence when facing
· Fixed an issue introduced in the previous hotfix causing command inputs to sometimes not queue up as you cleared an Encounter
· Other minor fixes

If you haven't already played through this title, you can find out more in our review here on Nintendo Life. Here's a quick sample:

"Supergiant Games is five-for-five with Hades II, a huge, triumphant sequel that manages to diverge mechanically and offer more challenges and variety than its predecessor. It helps that it’s nearly faultless on Switch 2 in particular."

Last week, Supergiant Games also announced the Hotfix 3 patch, you can find out more about this update in our previous post.

Have you played Hades II on the Switch or Switch 2 yet? What other updates would you like to see for this title? Let us know in the comments.

[source store.steampowered.com]

Liam is a news writer and reviewer on Nintendo Life. He's been writing about games for more than 15 years and is a lifelong fan of many iconic video game characters.

item.title: XBOX Reevaluating Next-Generation Console Amid Cost Concerns
item.content:encoded:
Matthew Ball, Xbox’s Chief Strategy Officer, comments on the brand rethinking its approach to Project Helix. Project Helix is the code name for XBOX’s next console. It’s planned to be a PC hybrid. However, the company is now reevaluating its approach to the new console as memory costs remain uncertain for the near future.

During a recent interview, Ball spoke about how the company continues to evaluate its approach to its next-generation console. Ultimately, the goal is not to ask too much of players, but also not to short investors.

XBOX Executive Discusses Changes to Project Helix Strategy

Speaking with Christopher Dring of The Game Business, Ball said they want to make sure the console is Affordable and are trying very hard to rethink what video game consoles can be. Not to exclude players but to have more than ever. While everything is no longer an XBOX, they still want to be the number-one console by 2030.

“We are working very hard to rethink everything that we can about Helix, which is a console we are committed to shipping, and we are very cognisant of the ways in which we need to change as a company to make sure it is affordable, to make sure that it’s flexible. We are working hard to rethink what the console model can look like, not in an exclusionary way, but in an additive way.”

Recently, some hard times hit Xbox as it was announced that they are preparing for Mass layoffs by July 2026. Learn more about that at this link.

Insider Gaming will continue to monitor all things from Microsoft’s gaming side and report any updates.

How do you feel about XBOX in 2026? Sound off about this story and all others in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

item.title: Tokyo Valkyries launches July 9
item.content:encoded:
Roguelite deckbuilder Tokyo Valkyries will launch for Switch and PC via Steam on July 9 for $22.99 / ¥2,980, developer qureate announced. It will support English, Japanese, Traditional Chinese, and Simplified Chinese language options.

About

Build the deck that saves Tokyo!

Tokyo Valkyries is a roguelite deckbuilder where players craft a deck and strategize to defeat the enemies that stand in their way and progress through the map.

—One day, an alternate world invading our reality, an Underworld, appears in Tokyo.

Only a set of chosen girls can stand against this anomaly.

By day, they lead their daily lives as regular university students; by night, they venture into the Underworld as warriors, fighting to protect the city of Tokyo.

Can this band of girls leading double lives stop the encroaching threat…?

Construct your deck and wield it to defend Tokyo from the Underworld invasion!

Story

Grand Earth, a world of swords and magic, was trampled at the hands of Belzebia, a dark spirit wielding unfathomable power.

In time, the shadow of her conquest began to creep towards even our reality, where magic does not exist…

Unaware of such a threat, Haruka Fuchi, a university student in Tokyo, is led by a voice crying for help and steps into a strange space.

Where she ended up was an Underworld, a space in between Grand Earth and our world, created by Belzebia.

In this Underworld, replete with the presence of darkness, Haruka meets a girl who introduces herself as Artesia.

If the Underworld is not sealed and Belzebia stopped, the world will be led into annihilation…

Now aware of the threat facing her world, Haruka would venture into the abyss of darkness with her allies.

Turn-Based Card Combat Where Every Choice Can Sway Your Fortunes!

Combat gameplay is comprised of turn-based card battles, where you fight with a deck you craft yourself! Each playable character has one of four Attributes—Resolve, Courage, Hope, and Dawn—and will take action when a card corresponding to their Attribute is played.

During your turn, enemy characters’ intended actions will be displayed to you. Based on this, the decision of whether to attack or harden your defenses becomes critical.

Understanding your opponent’s actions to counter with your offensive and defensive capabilities is the key to victory.

Select Your Own Path to Progress to the Boss and Seal its Underworld!

While engaged in a run, Players will encounter seven types of tiles on the map: Battles (fight regular enemies), Elites (challenge powerful enemies), Shops (purchase cards and items), Cafes (restore stamina or boost cards), Lockers (obtain random cards or items), Anomalies (encounter various events), and Bosses (defenders of the Cursed Swords).

The decision to take or skip the cards you see through battles and events is key.

Craft your own unique deck and venture into the depths to defeat bosses!

Listening to a Girl’s Instructions, Yank and Pull to Save the World!

In order to seal an Underworld its Cursed Sword, the source of its power, must be pulled out to purify the world. Follow Artesia’s instructions to pull on the Cursed Swords and stop the Underworld’s invasion.

These rituals are only for purification purposes, so don’t get any wrong ideas and make sure your imagination (among other things) doesn’t get inflated!

Pull One Out to Release the Girls’ True Powers!

When you pull out a Cursed Sword, its power can greatly strengthen one of your girls. The selected party member will gain one additional attribute and can be chosen as your party’s leader!

Girls chosen as leaders gain access to a super powerful special skill.

The combination of your party composition and deck building is the key to victory!

Feature Comparisons

The features listed below will look different depending on the platform.

  • Hand movement for the event CG of drawing the Cursed Sword.
    • Switch – She gently draws the Cursed Sword, with a soft touch.
    • PC – She forcefully draws the Cursed Sword, with a firm grip.

Characters

  • Haruka Fuchi (voiced by Yuki Nakashima) – A university student in Tokyo. Although she presents a cool and calm exterior, not exhibiting much emotion, on the inside she harbors strong passion. Because of her cold personality and (somewhat) stoic attitude, Haruka has always been relatively secluded, being seen by those around her as too good to be approached. Because of this, during her childhood Haruka experienced some sense of isolation. However, that does not mean she is bad at socializing. When relied on, Haruka can be kind and considerate.
  • Artesia (voiced by Moe Kakara) – A mage from another world. Artesia had been engaged in a solitary battle to foil the ambitions of Belzebia, fighting in the Underworld where our reality and alternate worlds intersect, when she meets Haruka and asks for her help. While normally an innocent and meek person, when it comes to the origins of her world and its impacts on Haruka’s reality, she thinks much more critically and gravely. Deeply respecting her elder sister Rozamia, ever since she was a child Artesia strived to follow in Rozamia’s footsteps. She came to Tokyo from her world in order to avenge her sister’s death at the hands of Belzebia.
  • Tsubasa Hikami (voiced by Mano Ayumi) – A student attending the same university as Haruka. Bright and cheerful, Tsubasa is the life of any party, always using her good-naturedness to make those around her smile. While she has a bit of a ditzy side, at her core Tsubasa harbors a strong resolve, not being one to easily give up. After having her interest piqued by Haruka’s way of doing everything on her own, Tsubasa began to frequently strike conversation with her, eventually becoming close friends. One of the very few people Haruka can open up to and speak her mind.
  • Anna Keijima (voiced by Eri Suzuki) – A student attending the same university as Haruka. A sociable and flashy gyaru. In contrast to her showy appearance, Anna is a caring person who deeply values her friendships. Obsessed with fashion and modelling, Anna shows understanding to people who also have things they are passionate about. Decided to pursue modelling after happening to come across a certain model’s photo shoot. Although her father strongly objected her pursuing modelling as a career, Anna managed to persevere and began working as an amateur model.
  • Mamika Kanesaki (voiced by Hikaru Iida) – A student attending the same university as Haruka. Hailing from an affluent family, Mamika is a prim and proper lady. Also academically gifted. Although intelligent and a critical thinker, her pride makes her bad at asking others for help. Due to her intense education, Mamika became a heavily result-oriented person. She respects her parents and their societal success, and works hard for their validation. While she is prideful of her prestigious background, Mamika is also fascinated by everyday people’s lives. Particularly, she enjoys unpretentious foods like instant noodles and junky snacks.
  • Raimu Shirogane (voiced by Rika Abe) – A student attending the same university as Haruka, in the year below her. Although she does not cross paths with Haruka and her friends often, Raimu is infamous in their university as an unhinged character. While usually well-behaved, Raimu talks to herself often and can take bold and surprising actions at times. A kind person at her core, Raimu has a fragile heart and becomes severely depressed at even small things. Due to having many negative social experiences, Raimu is prone to pessimism and is quick to shift blame onto others.
  • …and more!

Staff

  • Character Design: MIZU, Waon Inui
  • Scenario: inkedbrain Inc.
  • Producer: Yujiro Usuda

item.title: NAMCO LEGENDARY Mountains launches June 25
item.content:encoded:
• PC
  • Switch
  • Switch 2

Nintendo eShop pre-orders open on June 11.

3D puzzle game NAMCO LEGENDARY Mountains will launch for Switch 2, Switch, and PC via Steam on June 25 for 2,480 yen, developer BeXide announced. Pre-orders will open via Nintendo eShop on June 11 with a 10 percent discount.

Here is an overview of the game, via its Steam page:

About

Classic Namco arcades, reborn as a 3D puzzle! 80s legends return in a brand-new form!

Classic Pixel Art Meets 3D: Namco’s Iconic Characters Are Reborn as “Voxels”!

The world of legendary arcade games returns as a 3D puzzle! Toss, stack, and merge those “Voxels”—aim for the ultimate high score!

How to Play

Simple controls. Just toss the Voxel capsules into the field! When two voxels of the same kind collide, they transform into a larger one. Keep them on the field—don’t let them fall!

Key Features

  • Immerse Yourself in Classic Titles with Dedicated Stages and Sounds – Alongside a main stage that blends elements from multiple titles, you can enjoy dedicated stages for all-time classics: DIG DUG, PAC-MAN, MAPPY, THE TOWER OF DRUAGA, and XEVIOUS. Beyond just the visuals, the iconic music will take your gameplay to the next level!
  • Collect Over 100 Unique Voxels – Clear various challenges to keep unlocking new ones! Build your favorite lineup and enjoy a fully customized experience!
  • Build Your Own Gallery – Showcase your collection in the “Collection Room.” Arrange them freely to recreate your favorite memories or build the ultimate crossover universe!
  • Aim for the Top in Score Attack! – Relive the thrill of carving your name into the arcade high score board. Compete in online rankings against players worldwide to claim the top spot!

item.title: Deltarune Chapter 6 Is Progressing Faster Than Previous Chapters
item.content:encoded:
There is an update on Deltarune Chapter 6 from Toby Fox.

Deltarune Chapter 5 was announced during the June 2026 Nintendo Direct presentation, and Fox has already provided an update on the chapter that will come after. The news was shared via a newsletter following the announcement of Chapter 5.

Toby Fox Says Deltarune Chapter 6 Is Progressing Well

In the newsletter, Fox revealed that work on Chapter 6 is already coming along well. The following updates were shared: Cutines are making good progress, and all of the basic gimmicks of the chapter have been created. Now, development is focusing on creating more rooms to utilize the gimmicks at play in the chapter. Work on normal enemies and bullets is mostly complete, while work on the bullets of the final battle in the chapter is still progressing. It is noted that this chapter is easier to create than others, resulting in faster development. It’s also possible that work on chapter 7 could begin before the end of 2026.

This game is the successor to Undertale, and all current chapters of the game are available on every platform, including PC. The first couple of chapters were released back in 2018 for free. Fans then waited 7 years for the release of the third and fourth chapters, but the next three chapters are coming much faster. Chapter 5 will be available beginning June 24. Learn more about that here.

Insider Gaming will continue to monitor all things Deltarune and report on any news that surfaces. Be sure to check out everything announced during the June 2026 Nintendo Direct here.

How do you feel about Deltarune’s upcoming chapters? Sound off about this story and all others in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

item.title: The Lost Wild May Be the Dinosaur Horror Game You're Looking For
item.content:encoded:
You are part of the food chain in The Lost Wild. We had the chance to see a thirty minute demo, which was incredibly tense and showed the terrifying reality of what it would be like with dinosaurs around.

It was made clear early and stated several times that The Lost Wild is more of a tense cat and mouse game, not one in which you're going to be fighting dinosaurs. You're there to observe, adapt, and survive.

The tone is set early when in the demo we ran across some sort of researcher in a hazmat suit that gets spooked and runs right into a dinosaur. The dinosaur promptly picked the person up, chomped them in half, and the gory half of their body left hit the ground in front of us as we hid under a van. The dinosaur then picked it up and walked off.

Much of the demo was spent hidden in the brush observing and listening for the dinosaur that we had just seen eat someone. Every so often the loud thuds of it stomping in the forest could be heard, and most of the time we didn't see the dinosaur. Each stop was ominous and immediately upped the tension.

From the moment we saw the dinosaur, the feeling of the demo switched. Heart rate went up a bit, we were on alert, and all the classic markers of a horror game were present. In this case though, the big monster is a dinosaur we know can snap us up without much effort.

Developer Great Ape Games emphasized that this is just what dinosaurs would behave like, they're not trying to videogameify the dinosaurs in some way but just to naturally portray the fact that they're predators and you're prey.

While we didn't see them, you will eventually get some tools to "fight back." They didn't offer details, however.

All we really saw was the ability to pick up and throw the standard objects like glass bottles to make a sound and distract the dinosaur. Like, say, if you have a keycard and need to get it into a machine but need the dinosaur far enough away.

To up the more classic horror feel, you can only manually save if you have a save disk on you. They are found throughout the world and the machines to slot them in are scattered as well. They made sure to note that the fact you start putting in a disk isn't enough to save you, as there's a bit of a process and animation before the saving occurs. So, you'll need to be relatively safe, and it won't save you at the last second. Plan to die frequently.

As far as a demo presentation goes, The Lost Wild was great. The tension, the fear permeating from the screen, the overwhelming sounds… it accomplished its goal in making for a stressful experience.

With how common fodder dinosaurs are as enemies in games, it is refreshing to see them as truly terrifying as they actually would be if encountered in real life.

Survival horror fans should keep a close eye on The Lost Wild, especially if you're tired of the zombie aesthetic.

Andrew is the Editor in Chief at TechRaptor. Conned into a love of gaming by Nintendo at a young age, Andrew has been chasing the dragon spawned by… More about Andrew



https://techraptor.net/gaming/previews/lost-wild-may-be-dinosaur-horror-game-youre-looking-for

item.title: Xbox CEO Asha Sharma and executive vice president Matt Booty publish staff memo ‘Next 100 Days: Xbox Reset’
item.content:encoded:
Xbox CEO Asha Sharma and Xbox executive vice president and chief content officer Matt Booty have published a memo sent to Xbox staff today, discussing the company’s first 100 days under new leadership, the challenges facing the Xbox business, and its plans to “reset for a stronger Xbox.”

The memo paints a bleak outlook—despite over $20 billion in investment over the past five years, excluding Activision Blizzard King, the business’ core revenue has declined and is expected to end the fiscal year on June 30 with an approximately three percent margin, suggesting it may require significant restructuring.

Bloomberg‘s Jason Schreier also reported that Xbox is planning “major” layoffs shortly after the close of the fiscal year, citing people familiar with the company’s strategy, as well as significant cuts to marketing and other areas of the business.

Get the full message from Sharma and Booty below.

Team,

Over the first 100 days together, we have started to revive XBOX.

Our platform teams have already shipped more updates in the last 100 days than during the prior year combined. We now have more active partners on XBOX than ever before. Our Game Pass team set to work fixing our offering and after 8+ months of decline, our service has started to grow again. And through Player Voice, we have a 24/7 channel to hear directly from players, creators, and developers.

With the XBOX Games Showcase and the return of FanFest, we brought together hundreds of millions of fans globally. We reintroduced exclusives with Gears of War: E-Day in 2026 and Clockwork Revolution in 2027. Players can continue to expect signature exclusives from us every year. In parallel, Playground Games reminded us that established franchises can achieve incredible new highs.

These results are early, but they demonstrate what is possible when we move faster, stay close to our community, and align behind a shared vision. We have made mistakes, and will continue to make them, but what matters is that we listen, learn, and adjust the course where needed. Remember, our fans are rooting for us.

Now we start the next 100 days. It is important to have both optimism and realism as we work to reset the business.

Here are the realities that we need to navigate:

#1: Over 1 billion players choose to play XBOX and our games each year, for a total of 72 billion hours across Console, PC, Mobile, and Streaming (excluding much of China and a few other properties). Our franchises are also among the largest and most beloved globally and are now breaking records in TV and film. Going forward, our competition is attention. There are more great games, TV series, franchises, creators, content formats, apps, etc., than ever before.

#2: We will end this fiscal year at about a 3% accountability margin, down year-over-year. Excluding Activision Blizzard King, over the past five years, we have spent over $20 billion on ongoing investments in our content, platform, and hardware subsidy, but our annual revenue has declined nearly half a billion during that time. Going forward, this cannot continue.

#3: We are in a hardware component crisis. When I joined as CEO in February, the price we paid for console storage components was over 2x as high as we paid last fall. These costs have since doubled again. And as we plan for the 2027 holiday season, we expect another significant increase, taking us over 5x the prices we paid only two years earlier. Memory costs have followed a broadly similar trajectory. While the entire industry is facing a components crisis, we believe we have been impacted more greatly than many of our peers due to the choices we made over the last half decade. We are currently unable to make as many consoles as players want to buy, and we need a new business model and partnerships for hardware as we remain committed to Helix.

#4: We expanded our studio system when we needed a pipeline of content to meet multiple strategies across subscription, streaming, and devices. In the process, we have found ourselves over extended as we executed on changing strategies in a landscape of more readily available content. We are the fortunate stewards of industry-defining franchises that have enormous potential and player demand, but we have not adequately funded them to compete and win. At the same time, as we saw this past weekend at Showcase, a reliable pipeline of first- and third-party exclusives and new IP are critical to our success. We need to reassess the balance between these and our investment priorities for the next 5 years.

#5: Our current platform infrastructure is not built for the battle ahead. Our systems are overly complex, spanning hundreds of dependencies, which hinders our ability to move fast. We’ve become too reliant on vendors to operate our systems and must become more self-reliant as an engineering culture to build for the future. We must increase the value we ship to players while decreasing the time it takes to do so. Going forward, we’ll evolve and rebuild our stack and look at capabilities across all of XBOX and potential M&A to help us win in hardware, PC, mobile, and streaming.

For some of you, these realities will be surprising and even frustrating to discover. We won’t succeed by hiding hard truths, nor will we succeed by doing the same thing and expecting different results. Like the ‘everyday wins’ mentality from the first 100 days, we will sprint to make progress against hardware, content, experience, and services together.

XBOX is one of the few places where people come not just to play, but to connect with others to create memories. With console at the center of how our showcase experiences are defined, Windows as one of the largest gaming platforms in the world, and incredible games under our roof as one of the largest publishers in the world, we have the foundation in place.

Let’s reset for a stronger XBOX and build the #1 gaming and entertainment company.

Asha and Matt



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/06/xbox-ceo-asha-sharma-and-executive-vice-president-matt-booty-publish-staff-memo-next-100-days-xbox-reset
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Out of Words is A Beautiful Expression of Human Creativity and Art
item.content:encoded:
In a world where human creativity is genuinely under threat by AI, something like Out of Words existing as an undeniably human made passion project is amazing to see.

Out of Words is a co-op puzzle platformer that may be one of the most visually striking games ever made. That feels hyperbolic, but seeing the game in motion and just checking out almost any frame of a trailer, and you'll see what I mean.

Everything on the screen comes from a model or physical object crafted by human hands. The animations, facial expressions -- everything comes from the classic stop-motion tradition. Stop-motion films, even short films, in this style are a rarity and to see a game get this level of treatment is incredible.

If you look at the details on-screen close enough, you'll see what things are made of. The actual fabric the character's wear, the ribbons of thin colored plastic that make up floating seaweed underwater... the list is endless.

Talking to the development team, they talked about how in some ways their studio space is like any other: a bunch of PCs with unreal loaded up on them working on the game. But, in what seems like a more magical place, there's a huge space dedicated to crafting the models and world of Out of Words by hand, full of clays, fabrics, and all sorts of materials.

That may seem like a random aside, but it was a wonderful detail to hear when the more trailers and games that get released, the less certain we are of the generative AI usage in its development.

So, suffice to say, Out of Words is breathtakingly beautiful and shows some of the highest level of craft in the stop-motion medium, genuinely rivaling that of places like the legendary filmmaker Laika.

Out of Words takes place in a surreal world our heroes Kurt and Karla fall into because of their inability to talk with each other. They're deep friends who are moving onto a new chapter of their lives apart and neither of them wants to talk about the feelings they have for the other. That leads to their mouths disappearing and descending into this world.

The story is a very metaphorical one all about their relationship and running away from their worries and not having that frank conversation with one another. Huge monsters and black terrifying blobs are stand-ins for their anxiety, worries, and fears.

Throughout, there are a dozen or so gameplay mechanics in the journey. For our preview, we saw two. In one, it was your simple flipping of gravity, up was down sort of thing.

The other, also gravity related, saw us tossing back a little creature who, when he was with one character, would flip their gravity for them. So, it made for these trust fall moments where one of us would have to take a leap of faith and hope our partner tossed it over to swap the gravity and save ourselves.

In a particularly beautiful moment, we floated through air tossing the creature back and forth as we fell up and down in tandem.

Those are the only two gravity affecting mechanics, the rest throughout the game will be something different. They tested out hundreds of mechanics and settled on what's in the game due to how well they suited mirroring the relationship of Kurt and Karla. 

Out of Words Preview | Final Thoughts

The gameplay wasn't overly complicated, nor was it difficult, and it doesn't plan to be. Accessibility is a key pillar of the design, and developers Kong Orange and Wiredfly are hoping that Out of Words may be a great game to pick up with kids and non-gamers alike.

To some, those may be the words that turn them off from the game. But, from the brief snippet of the game and conversation I was able to have, the intention and metaphor behind the visuals and mechanics is what I find exciting. Blending it all in clever ways to further the story and give us insight into Kurt and Karla's relationship in a way only games can is sometimes even more exciting than the best combat systems out there.

Out of Words is for people that like the idea of a "vibes" game, where you're just there to soak up the experience. And, in this case, we'll get to soak in an experience with one of the most visually beautiful games out there.

Out of Words was previewed at Summer Game Fest Play Days.

Andrew is the Editor in Chief at TechRaptor. Conned into a love of gaming by Nintendo at a young age, Andrew has been chasing the dragon spawned by… More about Andrew

item.title: Report: Ubisoft Announces More Studio Closures And Layoffs Amid New Organizational Restructuring
item.content:encoded:
Ubisoft has announced a new wave of organizational restructurings resulting in the closures of two studios and hundreds of layoffs. 

As reported by Insider Gaming, the publisher sent an internal message to staff stating how it has been evolving to “simplify how it operates, reduce its cost base and strengthen the company for the long term.” To that end, it has shuttered its studios in Winnipeg and Belgrade.

Ubisoft Winnipeg opened its doors in January 2019 and served as a support studio for titles such as Rainbow Six Mobile, Far Cry 6, and Assassin's Creed Valhalla. Winnipeg's closure has reportedly impacted 65 employees. Ubisoft Belgrade had existed since 2016 and worked on several titles, including the recently revealed Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced. Roughly 100 employees are reportedly impacted.

Additionally, layoffs have been announced at Ubisoft Barcelona (51 employees) and Ubisoft San Francisco (the exact number of impacted employees is unknown, but was described by Insider Gaming as being “dozens”). The publisher also announced plans to restructure the Barcelona studio to focus on Rainbow Six projects. In total, Ubisoft’s internal message states that up to 380 employees may be affected, according to the message obtained by Insider Gaming. 

Today’s news is the latest development in a company-wide restructuring that has persisted since 2024. It follows the formation of subsidiary Vantage Studios to manage the Assassin’s Creed, Far Cry, and Rainbow Six franchises and an organizational “reset” announced in January that led to several game cancellations, including the long-awaited remake of Prince of Persia: The Sands of Time.

In the past two years, Ubisoft has shuttered studios in Leamington and Halifax and disbanded the internal team responsible for the well-received Prince of Persia: The Lost Crown due to underwhelming sales. Ubisoft also proposed layoffs at studios such as The Division developer Massive Entertainment and Trials developer RedLynx. Ghost Recon developer Red Storm Entertainment lost 100 employees in March and, while still in operation, has ceased active game development.

Today's news comes after Summer Game Fest, where the publisher showcased upcoming titles such as Assassin's Creed Black Flag Resynced and Rayman Legends Retold. While it has other Assassin's Creed projects in the works, as well as the long-in-development Beyond Good & Evil 2 and Splinter Cell remake, it's unclear what Ubisoft has in the pipeline in the foreseeable future. 

Game Informer has contacted Ubisoft for comment and will update this story should we receive one. 



https://gameinformer.com/2026/06/10/report-ubisoft-announces-more-studio-closures-and-layoffs-amid-new-organizational

item.title: Control Resonant Sets You Loose as a Powerful Agent of Chaos
item.content:encoded:
When I saw that we were finally getting the sequel to Control, I never would have thought it would be a more open world hack and slash RPG, featuring combat in the vein of like Devil May Cry. I am so glad they made the choice with Control Resonant.

It's been seven years since Control (as an aside, I learned all Remedy games are set in the year they come out, who knew?), and just has much time has passed in the game world. Jesse has been director of the Federal Bureau of Control for a minute and… things aren't going well.

The Hiss have broken containment and overrun the bureau, eventually breaking out of The Old House altogether. Through means I'll let you learn yourself, Jesse's brother Dylan also gets released. Finally, we get to see the power everyone is so afraid of.

Control Resonant is the biggest departure from what is the usual more measured, slow-paced combat from Remedy's previous games. The arsenal they've given Dylan fully embraces that turn to fast-paced action.

The moment to moment gameplay is twitchy, combo-driven, and rewards fast reactions. Mid-combo dodges are a key to survival, and quickly zipping from one enemy to the next as a moving target makes for some beautiful controlled chaos.

Your arsenal consists of a light weapon, heavy weapon, combo finisher, and a slate of three abilities. All of these are customizable and change up the playstyle quite a bit.

The light attack is your "main" weapon in a sense, the one you'll be slashing with the most and those feel as you'd expect. They vary with effects, like one is lower damage with faster attacks and higher crit chance and another does more damage when hitting someone from behind.

One heavy attack is a massive hammer that smashes down, while another is a long bladed whip that lashes out at enemies and can hit in a big arc.

Combo finishers actually change quite a bit of the combat too. These are finishers that start up at the end of a light attack combo. One was a series of quick jabs that increased enemy stagger and another was this wide hard-hitting cleave.

Abilities were all over the place from a shield of earth, to slamming down on the ground causing a small shockwave, and even one where you shoot out a big burst of fire.

I bring all of that up to say, there is a lot you can do to customize Dylan's arsenal to play the way you want. And there's even a talent tree to vary the playstyles even more.

Another departure from the norm for Remedy, Resonant has large open spaces to explore. It's not exactly full on open-world, but there are large sandboxes to run around in and explore with a variety of enemies and events to partake in.

The world is a warped Manhattan that is stretched, flipped, endless, and, well, manipulated in anyway you can think of. Liminal spaces abound and the eerie floating bodies of the citizenry float everywhere.

I was able to explore a part of one of those open areas, and they are absolutely huge. There's a lot to see both horizontally and vertically, and Resonant gives you an incredible amount of tools to get just about anywhere you want.

Dylan can double jump, hold that jump to go up just a little higher and float for a bit to glide further, can dodge twice in the air to dash forward, and you can get a good sprint going before you go into a jump to get even further.

The distances you can travel are immense, with moments in the prologue that show you these jumps looking impossible. It's no wonder the FBC saw so much potential in Dylan.

Resonant gives you a level of freedom I would never have expected in a Control sequel. The original wasn't necessarily on-rails, but Resonant is really making this corrupted version of Manhattan your playground to fight the Hiss.

Control Resonant Preview | Final Thoughts

I greatly enjoyed my time with Control Resonant. The gameplay is focused, satisfying, and twitchy in the best ways. The world is just as weird as it was before, and I know I'll be in for a story with some awesome twists and turns.

Control Resonant releases in the very busy month of September, and I think this is one that should be towards the top of your lists.

Control Resonant was previewed at Summer Game Fest Play Days.

Andrew is the Editor in Chief at TechRaptor. Conned into a love of gaming by Nintendo at a young age, Andrew has been chasing the dragon spawned by… More about Andrew



https://techraptor.net/gaming/previews/control-resonant-sets-you-loose-as-powerful-agent-of-chaos

item.title: This Cannot Continue
item.content:encoded:
Xbox CEO Asha Sharma and Xbox Game Studios head Matt Booty just sent an email out to all Xbox employees with a clear, yet terrifying message: “this cannot continue.”

Shared publicly via Xbox Wire, the email paints a picture of a broken division, bogged down by the weight of both years of unsuccessful investments and unchecked excess, and battered by the winds of outside economic forces. Sharma, now having been in charge for 100 days, has made it clear that what she is spearheading is indeed going to be a hard reset, complete with hard decisions that will make or break the division and ripple out through the lives of its thousands of employees.

Sharma and Booty outline a number of harsh “realities” that they say the company will need to navigate in the 100 days to come. The current fiscal year, ending June 30, will see Xbox at about a 3-percent profit margin—that’s wildly low. The company has spent $20 billion on studio investments in five years, not including its $69 billion purchase of Activision Blizzard King, and its annual revenue has in turn declined nearly half a billion. The company is currently paying 4x as much for components as it did last fall, and it expects to be paying even more by holiday 2027.

The email calls the company’s massive studio system “over extended,” saying the company tried too hard to juggle too many different platform strategies and has “not adequately funded” its studios to be competitive. And it says that its platform infrastructure is “overly complex,” not “self-reliant,” and “not built for the battle ahead.”

Yeah. I’d say they’re right. This cannot continue.

This email is a brutal indictment of the series of decisions and events that led Xbox to where it is today. It condemns just about every strategy the company has attempted over the last decade and change, from its relentless gobbling-up of a huge chunk of the industry’s dwindling AA space only to fail to adequately support most of it, to its endless flip-flopping between a focus on consoles to an emphasis on subscription services to turning everything into an Xbox. Its spending, it seems, has been out of control, and it has little to show for it. Sharma and Booty are claiming to be the ones to fix it, though it’s worth noting that Booty himself was part of the team that oversaw it almost a decade.

I don’t doubt that this is going to be a challenging transition for Sharma, Booty, and everyone on their team. And I genuinely hope they succeed. I really do. Not because I care about Microsoft the corporation or its profit margins, but because I think the devs working for Xbox make cool things, many of which I’d like to play one day, and I want these talented, creative people to be able to keep making cool art for all of us to enjoy.

But that’s just it, right? That’s the bit that’s missing here from Sharma and Booty’s message. Not present anywhere here is a whisper of who, ultimately, is going to be facing these harsh realities. I’m sure Sharma and Booty have challenging jobs, trying to sort all this out. But what we learned just moments ago is that Xbox is, as a part of this reset, heading for mass layoffs. Of course it is. We’ve known this was coming for months. Any time an executive changeover happens, any time a new leader says it’s time for a “reset,” this is what happens. They make a bunch of changes to the corporate structure, for financial good or ill, and tens, or hundreds, or thousands of people lose their livelihoods. Some will suffer permanent setbacks in their lives as a result of this. Some will leave the game industry forever. Some will lose health coverage at critical times, or be unable to pay mortgages, or lose visa support at a time when the U.S. specifically is making it harder than ever to live here if you don’t look a certain way.

Sharma and Booty don’t name names in this email as to whose fault this all is, despite the pretty extreme finger-pointing that seems to be bubbling under the surface here. But I don’t think it ultimately matters which specific executive(s) anyone blames this on, whether it’s the past administration’s decisions or those of the current one, whether it’s the Xbox division itself or its highly-profitable parent Microsoft. It’s all of them. It’s the whole thing. The whole damn system that has led to the wanton flinging around of millions and billions of dollars just to own intellectual property and manage or mismanage teams of creatives. This industry is about to screw over another big group of individuals who didn’t do anything to deserve it, and there doesn’t seem to be anything that can stop it. Ubisoft laid off hundreds just earlier today. Over 20,000 game devs have been laid off in the last two and a half years, more layoffs are on the way, and the hits keep coming, all because no one seems to be interested in managing this industry sustainably, because sustainable doesn’t mean “massively profitable for shareholders.”

This cannot continue. I just don’t know when it’s going to finally stop.



https://kotaku.com/this-cannot-continue-xbox-layoffs-2000705049

item.title: Xbox Reportedly Planning More Mass Layoffs This Month as CEO Asha Sharma Admits Xbox is “Over Extended”
item.content:encoded:
More mass layoffs are set to hit the video game industry, as Xbox is reportedly planning another mass layoff at the end of the month, according to a new report from Bloomberg's Jason Schreier. It's unclear how many employees will be impacted at this point, though an email referenced in the report attributed to Sharma, and recent public comments from Sharma point to it being a significant number of employees.

It'll be made public on June 30, 2026, according to the report at the end of Microsoft's current fiscal year. This comes after Sharma admitted that the Xbox division "isn't particularly healthy," and the company's chief strategy officer, Matthew Ball, was frank about how the company will have to rethink its hardware approach amid rising component costs.

Shortly after the Bloomberg report went live, an Xbox Wire blog post titled 'Next 100 Days: Xbox Reset' went live with the same quotes listed in Schreier's report, along with the notice at the top that the entire text of the post had just been emailed to Xbox staff globally. The post does not make mention of layoffs, but it does nail down the point that Xbox cannot continue in its current form.

"We will end this fiscal year at about a 3% accountability margin, down year-over-year," the post reads. "Excluding Activision Blizzard King, over the past five years, we have spent over $20 billion on ongoing investments in our content, platform, and hardware subsidy, but our annual revenue has declined nearly half a billion during that time. Going forward, this cannot continue."

"We expanded our studio system when we needed a pipeline of content to meet multiple strategies across subscription, streaming, and devices. In the process, we have found ourselves over extended as we executed on changing strategies in a landscape of more readily available content. We are the fortunate stewards of industry-defining franchises that have enormous potential and player demand, but we have not adequately funded them to compete and win. At the same time, as we saw this past weekend at Showcase, a reliable pipeline of first- and third-party exclusives and new IP are critical to our success. We need to reassess the balance between these and our investment priorities for the next 5 years."

It's no secret that Xbox revenue has been in decline recently. Sharma's comments only reiterate what we know from reading quarterly reports, the most recent of which reported that Xbox's overall revenue was down 5% while hardware revenue was down by over 30% for the second straight quarter. Sharma even admitted at the time of that report that while the division had made some positive traction, the reality was that "player and revenue growth has not yet met our ambition."

To see Xbox go through another round of mass layoffs will be the latest in an unfortunate series of events that game makers at Microsoft have had to endure. It's another sign of the industry's instability, and it's the second reported mass layoff today after Ubisoft shuttered more studios across the globe.

About the author: David has been writing about videogames, technology, and culture since 2020, with a focus on reporting daily news across multiple publications, including GameDaily.Biz, GameSkinny, and PlayStation Universe before joining Wccftech in 2025. David started contributing as Canada/US reporter for Wccftech's gaming section in 2025. Besides being up-to-date on the industry's movements, he loves interviewing developers, reviewing games, and writing intricate essays about the symbolism and layered meanings to be found in rich narratives as he's done for publications like GamesIndustry.Biz, LostInCult, and others. Outside of games he loves movies, music, theatre, his hometown, and his family, though not necessarily in that order.

Follow Wccftech on Google to get more of our news coverage in your feeds.



https://wccftech.com/xbox-mass-layoffs-asha-sharma/

item.title: Xbox warns of a ‘reset’ as it prepares for layoffs
item.content:encoded:
Microsoft’s Xbox division will be hit with significant layoffs next month, according to people familiar with Microsoft’s plans.

The company has been preparing for the layoffs internally for weeks, with Xbox CEO Asha Sharma hinting about “making hard choices” last month. Sources suggest the cuts could even involve a studio closure, or changes to the Xbox studio lineup. In a recent Giant Bomb episode, rumors of 1,000 layoffs for Microsoft’s Xbox division were mentioned. Bloomberg also reported today that the cuts would be “major,” and involve budget cuts for marketing and other areas of Microsoft’s Xbox business.

Moments before Bloomberg’s report, Xbox CEO Asha Sharma and chief content officer Matt Booty sent a memo to Xbox staff, warning of an “Xbox reset” over the next 100 days. Sharma and Booty pointed to difficult challenges the business is facing. “Excluding Activision Blizzard King, over the past five years, we have spent over $20 billion on ongoing investments in our content, platform, and hardware subsidy, but our annual revenue has declined nearly half a billion during that time,” they wrote. “Going forward, this cannot continue.”

They also noted that “we are in a hardware component crisis,” saying that component costs for the 2027 holiday season are expected to be “over 5x the prices we paid only two years earlier. Memory costs have followed a broadly similar trajectory.” They said that “we need a new business model and partnerships for hardware as we remain committed to Helix.”

Sharma and Xbox strategy chief Matthew Ball have been hinting at “radically different” console business models this week, and the mention of “partnerships for hardware” certainly plays into the idea that other PC OEMs might be able to create Xbox-branded devices based on AMD’s new chips in the future.

But right now, Xbox’s “current platform infrastructure is not built for the battle ahead,” Sharma and Booty wrote, saying that “we’ll evolve and rebuild our stack and look at capabilities across all of XBOX and potential M&A to help us win in hardware, PC, mobile, and streaming.”

Sharma has already made some big decisions since taking over, including making Gears of War: E-Day and Clockwork Revolution Xbox console exclusives.

Follow topics and authors from this story to see more like this in your personalized homepage feed and to receive email updates.

  • Tom Warren
  • Jay Peters
